title: Reports: Austin Reaves, Lakers agree to 4-year deal
summary: NBA.com, citing multiple reports led by ESPN's Shams Charania, reported that Austin Reaves plans to stay with the Lakers on a four-year, $185 million deal with a player option in the final season.

title: 5 takeaways: College players rule 1st round of 2026 NBA Draft
summary: NBA.com's first-round recap said the 2026 draft largely avoided major surprises while college players, especially freshmen, dominated the top of the board.

title: Kings trade back into first round, grab No. 29 pick
summary: NBA.com reported that Sacramento traded back into the first round to acquire No. 29 and use it on two-time NCAA champion Alex Karaban.

title: Nuggets opt for assets, trade No. 26 to Spurs
summary: NBA.com reported that Denver moved the No. 26 pick to San Antonio during Round 1, choosing future assets instead of making the selection.

title: Knicks, Lakers, Mavs and Suns complete Draft-night swap
summary: NBA.com reported that a four-team draft-night trade shuffled three first-rounders and sent additional second-round assets across New York, Los Angeles, Dallas and Phoenix.

title: Grizzlies swap picks with Thunder at No. 17, Pistons at 21
summary: NBA.com reported that Memphis traded down twice in the first round, dealing with Oklahoma City and Detroit to add multiple future second-round picks.

title: Wizards tab AJ Dybantsa with No. 1 overall pick in 2026 NBA Draft
summary: NBA.com reported that Washington used the top pick on BYU wing AJ Dybantsa, choosing him over fellow headline prospects Darryn Peterson and Cameron Boozer.

title: Blazers hire Wolves assistant Micah Nori as coach
summary: NBA.com reported that Portland hired longtime Timberwolves assistant Micah Nori as its new head coach, replacing interim coach Tiago Splitter.

title: Reports: Ayo Dosunmu to re-sign with Timberwolves
summary: NBA.com, citing ESPN's Shams Charania and the St. Paul Pioneer Press, reported that Ayo Dosunmu intends to return to Minnesota on a five-year, $112 million deal with a player option in the fifth season.

title: Reports: Heat acquire Giannis Antetokounmpo in blockbuster deal
summary: NBA.com, citing The Associated Press, reported that Miami is acquiring Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis from Milwaukee for Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis and draft assets.

title: Reports: Julius Randle traded to Nets on eve of draft
summary: NBA.com, citing multiple reports including ESPN's Shams Charania, reported that Minnesota is sending Julius Randle and No. 28 to Brooklyn in a three-team deal that also moves Nic Claxton to Chicago.

title: Reports: Trae Young, Wizards agree to 4-year deal
summary: NBA.com, citing ESPN's Shams Charania and The Athletic's Josh Robbins, reported that Trae Young will remain in Washington on a four-year deal worth about $212 million with a player option in Year 4.

title: Mavericks hire Michigan's Dusty May as coach
summary: NBA.com reported that Dallas officially hired Dusty May after his NCAA title run at Michigan, giving the Mavericks a new head coach for 2026-27.
